key_insight: Latent Drift forecasts slow disease progression by modeling anatomical change in a compressed latent space, reducing the tendency to copy baseline MRI anatomy and instead emphasizing subtle longitudinal drift.
clinical_relevance: If validated, this approach could help anticipate future neurodegenerative brain changes earlier, supporting more targeted monitoring, trial design, and intervention planning.
method: Learn progression in a latent semantic representation rather than predicting full-resolution MRI directly
Use finite scalar quantization to filter small high-frequency nuisance variation while retaining consistent structural change
Frame forecasting as progressive generative modeling to avoid identity collapse and diffuse spurious changes
evidence_signal: The abstract reports improved patient-specific neuro-forecasting on longitudinal 3D brain MRI versus diffusion and autoregressive transformer baselines across generative fidelity and clinically relevant metrics.
watch_item: Performance depends on accurate longitudinal data and careful validation, since low-signal progression can still be confounded by noise and the method may not generalize equally across diseases or imaging protocols.
paper_title: Progression as Latent Drift: Generative Forecasting of Slow-Evolving Pathologies
